methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Trilobio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Roya Amini-Naieni and Maximilian Schommer, having raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, and Abridge.

Of the three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. This method uses the company's $11M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in this sector, yielding an implied valuation of $28M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed absent revenue disclosure, which is standard for private-stage companies. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables—only 2 same-stage peers exist in the filtered universe, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability.

The triangulated range of $23M to $32M, centered on $28M, reflects reliance on a single methodological leg. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for Trilobio's specific sector dynamics and capital efficiency profile, and the absence of revenue-based valuation anchors. The range should be treated as directional pending additional data on unit economics or disclosed funding rounds from comparable-stage peers.
